decisions: Kept all 6 daily logs (04-21 through 04-26): verified by date arithmetic that none are 7+ days old. 04-21 (6 days old) becomes deletion candidate on 04-28.

No content promoted to any tier: reviewed all daily logs — each contains only consolidation maintenance notes (bootstrap entry in 04-21, routine heartbeat entries in 04-22 through 04-26). No user-generated content, corrections, preferences, or learnings to extract.
observations: Brain has been idle for 7 days since bootstrap — all daily logs contain only maintenance heartbeat entries with no substantive content to promote
Memory tiers (core, semantic, episodic, procedural) remain empty except for 3 reflection entries in episodic — this is expected for an agent with no user interactions yet
Daily log coverage is at 100% (7/7 days) but all entries are maintenance-generated, not user-session-generated
recommendations: On 04-28 consolidation, delete memory/daily/2026-04-21.md — its content (bootstrap entry) is already preserved in memory/episodic/reflection-2026-04-21.md
If brain remains idle past day 10, consider reducing consolidation verbosity — current reports add overhead without new information
processImprovements: [self-critique] Seven consecutive days of idle-brain consolidation producing near-identical reports. The process is working correctly but generating overhead with no substantive content to process. Need to distinguish between 'idle maintenance' and 'active maintenance' to avoid report bloat.

[proposal] For idle brains (no user sessions in 48h+), consolidation could produce a minimal report noting 'no changes, still idle' rather than full-format reports. This would reduce noise while preserving the audit trail.
[blocked] The maintenance-guard.sh script is in base-brain but the path isn't configured for channel brains — had to use the base-brain absolute path. This should be standardized.
